home_services_page.faq1_question: How much does it cost to renovate a house in Rome?
home_services_page.faq1_answer: The cost of a home renovation in Rome ranges from €400 to €1,200 per sqm, depending on the type of work. A complete bathroom renovation starts from around €5,000, while a full renovation of an 80sqm apartment averages between €40,000 and €80,000. Edilquadro offers free, personalized quotes.
home_services_page.faq2_question: How long does it take to renovate an apartment?
home_services_page.faq2_answer: Timelines depend on the scope of work: a bathroom renovation takes about 2-3 weeks, while a complete apartment renovation typically takes 2-4 months. Edilquadro guarantees adherence to agreed timelines.
home_services_page.faq3_answer: Yes, Edilquadro offers a turnkey service that includes managing all building permits: CILA, SCIA, permits, and communications with the City of Rome. We also handle the paperwork for tax benefits.

building_services_page.faq1_question: How much does it cost to renovate a condominium in Rome?
building_services_page.faq1_answer: The cost of condominium renovation depends on the type of work: facade renovation starts from €50-80/sqm, thermal insulation from €80-120/sqm, roof renovation from €100-200/sqm. Edilquadro provides detailed quotes and support for the condominium resolution.
building_services_page.faq2_question: Can tax benefits be used for condominium renovation?
building_services_page.faq2_answer: Yes, condominiums can benefit from various tax incentives: the 50% renovation bonus, the 65% ecobonus for energy efficiency improvements, and the sismabonus for seismic upgrading. Edilquadro handles all the necessary documentation.
building_services_page.faq3_question: Does Edilquadro manage relations with the building administrator?
building_services_page.faq3_answer: Yes, Edilquadro manages relations directly with the building administrator, prepares quotes for the assembly meeting, keeps all residents updated on work progress, and ensures compliance with current regulations.

about_page.meta_title: About Us - Edilquadro | Construction Company Rome
about_page.meta_description: Discover Edilquadro: a construction company in Rome's Tuscolana area with over 25 years of experience in home, shop, and building renovations. Qualified team and 200+ completed projects.
about_page.title: About Us
about_page.subtitle: For over 25 years, Edilquadro has been the trusted partner for renovations in Rome. Based in the Tuscolana area, our team of qualified professionals transforms spaces into unique environments.
about_page.story_p1: Edilquadro was founded in 1998, born from Stylianos Kountalis's passion for construction and renovation. Starting as a small artisan company in Rome's Tuscolana area, we built our reputation project after project, caring for every detail with dedication and professionalism.
about_page.story_p2: Over the years, we expanded our team and expertise, becoming a full-service construction company capable of managing projects of any size: from single apartment renovations to the restoration of entire buildings and residential complexes.
about_page.story_p3: Today, Edilquadro operates throughout Rome and its province, with a portfolio of over 200 successfully completed projects and a consolidated network of professionals, architects, and designers who collaborate to offer tailor-made solutions to our clients.
about_page.value1_desc: We use only top-quality materials and cutting-edge construction techniques. Every project undergoes rigorous quality checks.
about_page.value2_desc: Detailed quotes, fixed timelines, and constant communication. With us, you always know what to expect, with no surprises.
about_page.value3_desc: Careful planning and efficient site management. We are committed to meeting the deadlines agreed upon with our clients.
about_page.value4_desc: We guide you from start to finish: design, permits, construction, and after-work support. One point of contact for everything.
about_page.area_desc: Our headquarters is in the Tuscolana area of Rome, but we operate across the entire city and province. From apartment renovations in the historic center to work on villas and residential complexes in surrounding municipalities.
about_page.cta_subtitle: Contact us for a free site visit and a personalized quote. We'll get back to you within 24 hours.

tuscolana.intro_p2: For over 25 years we have served residents and business owners in the area with quality renovations, reliable timelines, and transparent quotes. Our proximity allows for quick site visits and immediate assistance during work.

bonus_page.meta_title: Renovation Tax Benefits 2026 Rome - Edilquadro Guide
bonus_page.meta_description: Complete guide to renovation tax benefits in Italy 2026: 50% renovation bonus, 65% ecobonus, sismabonus, facade bonus. Edilquadro handles paperwork and construction in Rome.
bonus_page.subtitle: All the tax bonuses and deductions available for home renovation, energy efficiency improvement, and property renewal in Rome.
bonus_page.intro_p1: 2026 still offers significant opportunities for those looking to renovate their property while taking advantage of tax deductions provided by law. From construction renovation bonuses to energy efficiency incentives, the savings opportunities are concrete and substantial.
bonus_page.intro_p2: Edilquadro, with over 25 years of renovation experience in Rome, guides you in choosing the most suitable bonus for your project and manages all the necessary documentation. We accompany you from the feasibility analysis to the completion of works, ensuring you obtain every available tax benefit.
bonus_page.bonus_ristrutturazione_desc: 50% IRPEF deduction on building renovation expenses, spread over 10 years. Valid for extraordinary maintenance, restoration, conservative rehabilitation, and renovation of individual property units.
bonus_page.bonus_ristrutturazione_works: Bathroom renovation, kitchen, electrical and plumbing systems, partition walls, flooring, windows.
bonus_page.bonus_ecobonus_desc: Deductions for energy efficiency improvements: from 50% for windows and solar shading, to 65% for condensing boilers, heat pumps, and thermal insulation.
bonus_page.bonus_ecobonus_works: Window replacement, condensing boiler installation, heat pump, thermal insulation, solar panels.
bonus_page.bonus_sismabonus_desc: Deduction from 50% to 85% for seismic safety interventions on buildings in seismic risk zones 1, 2, and 3. Also includes interventions combining seismic risk reduction and energy efficiency.
bonus_page.bonus_sismabonus_works: Structural consolidation, pillar and beam reinforcement, seismic upgrading, foundation work.
bonus_page.bonus_facciata_desc: 60% deduction for recovery and restoration work on building facades. Includes cleaning, painting, and work on balconies, ornaments, and friezes.
bonus_page.bonus_facciata_works: Facade plastering, external painting, balcony restoration, cornices and gutters.
bonus_page.bonus_verde_desc: 36% IRPEF deduction on expenses for landscaping open areas, creating gardens, terraces, and irrigation systems.
bonus_page.bonus_verde_works: Gardens, green terraces, irrigation systems, green roofs, wells.
bonus_page.bonus_barriere_desc: 75% deduction for interventions aimed at overcoming and eliminating architectural barriers. Valid for existing buildings.
bonus_page.bonus_barriere_works: Elevator installation, freight lifts, ramps, automatic doors, disabled bathroom adaptation.
bonus_page.help_title: How We Help with Tax Bonuses
bonus_page.help_step1_desc: We visit your property to assess the necessary work and identify the applicable bonuses for your specific case.
bonus_page.help_step2_desc: We provide a clear quote indicating the cost of works, the deductible amount, and the actual tax savings.
bonus_page.help_step3_desc: We handle all necessary documentation: CILA/SCIA permits, energy certifications, ENEA communications, and traceable payments.
bonus_page.help_step4_desc: We carry out the work to the highest standards and deliver all documentation needed for your tax return.
bonus_page.faq_title: Frequently Asked Questions about Tax Bonuses
bonus_page.faq1_q: Can I use multiple bonuses for the same property?
bonus_page.faq1_a: Yes, it is possible to combine different bonuses for the same property, provided the works are different and expenses do not overlap. For example, you can use the renovation bonus for the bathroom and the ecobonus for windows.
bonus_page.faq2_q: How does payment work to obtain the tax deduction?
bonus_page.faq2_a: Payments must be made via a 'talking bank transfer' (bonifico parlante), which states the payment reason, the beneficiary's tax code, and the company's VAT number. Edilquadro guides you through every step.
bonus_page.faq3_q: Do I need to submit a building permit for the bonuses?
bonus_page.faq3_a: It depends on the type of work. For extraordinary maintenance, a CILA is required; for structural work, a SCIA. We handle the submission to the relevant Municipality.
bonus_page.faq4_q: Does Edilquadro handle the ENEA paperwork for the ecobonus?
bonus_page.faq4_a: Yes, we handle the ENEA communication within 90 days of work completion, including all necessary technical documentation (APE energy certificates, technical data sheets, etc.).
bonus_page.faq5_q: How long does it take to recover the tax deduction?
bonus_page.faq5_a: Deductions are spread over 10 equal annual installments in your tax return (730 or Modello Redditi). The savings begin from the first year after the works are completed.
bonus_page.cta_subtitle: Contact us for a free site visit: we'll analyze your case and identify the available bonuses for your project.

blog_art1.title: How Much Does It Cost to Renovate a House in Rome in 2026
blog_art1.meta_title: House Renovation Costs Rome 2026 - Price Guide | Edilquadro
blog_art1.excerpt: Complete guide to house renovation costs in Rome: prices per sqm, factors affecting cost, how to save with tax benefits.
blog_art1.content: Renovating a house in Rome is a major investment that requires careful planning. In this guide, we analyze real costs based on our 25+ years of experience in the industry.|||## Average Renovation Costs in Rome|||For a complete renovation in Rome, average costs in 2026 range from €600 to €1,200 per square meter. This wide range depends on several factors: the area of Rome, the condition of the property, the quality of materials chosen, and the complexity of the work.|||## Light Renovation (€400-600/sqm)|||Includes painting, floor replacement, basic bathroom renovation, and partial electrical system update. Ideal for apartments in good condition that need an aesthetic refresh.|||## Complete Renovation (€600-1,000/sqm)|||Includes internal demolitions, complete system renovation (electrical, plumbing, heating), new floors, two complete bathrooms, new windows, and painting. This is the most requested intervention by our clients.|||## Premium Renovation (€1,000-1,500/sqm)|||Premium materials, custom design, home automation, solid wood floors, designer fixtures, project lighting. Recommended for valuable properties in Rome's city center.|||## How to Save: Tax Benefits|||The 50% Renovation Bonus allows you to deduct half the expense over 10 years. For a €50,000 project, the effective saving is €25,000. The 65% Ecobonus covers energy efficiency work such as thermal insulation and boiler replacement.|||## Why Choose Edilquadro|||With over 500 completed projects in Rome, we offer transparent and detailed quotes. Our team follows you from design to key delivery, including all bureaucratic and tax paperwork. Contact us for a free site visit.

blog_art2.title: Bathroom Renovation in Rome: Complete Guide 2026
blog_art2.meta_title: Bathroom Renovation Rome - Costs & Complete Guide 2026 | Edilquadro
blog_art2.excerpt: Everything you need to know about renovating a bathroom in Rome: costs, timelines, 2026 trends, mistakes to avoid, and how to choose the right materials.
blog_art2.content: The bathroom is one of the most important rooms in the house, and its renovation can completely transform living comfort. Here is our complete guide based on hundreds of bathrooms renovated in Rome.|||## How Much Does a Bathroom Renovation Cost in Rome|||Costs vary based on size and finish level: a standard bathroom (4-6 sqm) costs between €5,000 and €12,000, while a designer bathroom can reach €15,000-20,000. The price includes demolition, waterproofing, systems, tiles, fixtures, and fittings.|||## 2026 Bathroom Trends|||2026 features large format tiles (120x60 cm), neutral and earthy colors, walk-in flush showers, wall-hung toilets, and integrated LED lighting. Natural stone effect porcelain stoneware is the most requested material.|||## Mistakes to Avoid|||Don't underestimate waterproofing: it's the most important phase to avoid future problems. Don't skimp on fixture and fitting quality. Always plan an electrical outlet near the mirror and adequate ventilation.|||## Timeline|||A complete bathroom requires an average of 15-20 working days. This includes: 2-3 days of demolition, 3-4 days for systems, 2 days of waterproofing, 4-5 days of tile laying, and 3-4 days for fixture installation and finishing.|||## Our Approach|||At Edilquadro we create turnkey bathrooms: from the initial site visit to material selection, from 3D design to completion. We also accompany you in choosing materials from our partner suppliers. Request a free quote for your bathroom.

blog_art3.title: Renovation Bonus 2026: How It Works and How to Apply
blog_art3.meta_title: Renovation Tax Bonus 2026 Italy - How It Works | Edilquadro Rome
blog_art3.excerpt: Practical guide to the 2026 Renovation Bonus in Italy: requirements, amounts, necessary documents, and how Edilquadro helps you get the tax deduction.
blog_art3.content: The Renovation Bonus is the most widely used tax incentive for construction work in Italy. Here's how it works in 2026 and how you can benefit for your renovation in Rome.|||## What Is the 2026 Renovation Bonus|||The Renovation Bonus provides a 50% IRPEF deduction on expenses for extraordinary maintenance, restoration, and renovation work. The deduction is spread over 10 equal annual installments, with a maximum spending cap of €96,000 per property unit.|||## Which Works Are Eligible|||The following are deductible: bathroom renovation, replacement of electrical and plumbing systems, floor renovation, partition demolition and reconstruction, window replacement, elevator installation, energy-saving measures, and much more. Ordinary maintenance on individual residential units is not eligible.|||## Required Documents|||To benefit from the bonus you need: ASL notification (if required), traceable payments via specific bank transfer with renovation reference, contractor invoices, and system compliance declarations. It is essential to keep all documentation for at least 15 years.|||## The Specific Bank Transfer|||Payment must be made via a specific renovation bank transfer. The transfer must contain: payment reason with legal reference, tax code of the deduction beneficiary, and tax code or VAT number of the contractor.|||## How Edilquadro Helps You|||Our team handles all bureaucratic procedures for you: from the CILA to the Rome Municipality, to preparing the specific bank transfers, to closing the works. We provide all necessary documentation for the tax deduction. With Edilquadro you don't have to worry about anything: we take care of everything.

blog_art4.title: Apartment Renovation in Rome: Complete Step-by-Step Guide
blog_art4.meta_title: Apartment Renovation Rome - Complete Guide 2026 | Edilquadro
blog_art4.excerpt: From design to handover: complete guide to apartment renovation in Rome. Permits, timelines, costs and practical tips from Edilquadro.
blog_art4.content: Renovating an apartment in Rome is an exciting but complex project. With over 500 completed renovations, Edilquadro guides you step by step through every phase of the process.|||## Phase 1: Design and Site Survey|||Everything starts with a free site survey at your apartment. Our technicians assess the property's condition, listen to your needs, and propose customized solutions. In this phase we define the layout, space distribution, materials, and preliminary budget.|||## Phase 2: Permits and Paperwork in Rome|||For a complete renovation in Rome you need a CILA (Certified Start of Works Communication), to be submitted to the relevant Municipality. Our technical office prepares all necessary documentation, including the qualified designer's technical report. Preparation takes approximately 7-10 days.|||## Phase 3: Demolition and Preparation|||Demolition includes removing existing floors, partitions to be modified, old systems, and wall coverings. This is an important phase that requires care to avoid damaging load-bearing structures. Average duration: 3-5 working days for an 80-100 sqm apartment.|||## Phase 4: Electrical and Plumbing Systems|||Complete system renovation is essential for safety and comfort. CEI-compliant electrical system with differential switch, LED light points, integrated USB outlets. Plumbing with multi-layer pipes, manifolds, and thermostatic mixers. Duration: 5-7 days.|||## Phase 5: Screeds and Flooring|||After systems installation, leveling screeds are laid and flooring is installed. Porcelain stoneware, parquet, resin: each material has different installation times. For a standard apartment, this phase requires 7-10 working days including drying times.|||## Phase 6: Bathrooms and Finishes|||Installation of sanitary ware, bathroom tiling, interior doors, skirting boards, and various finishes. This is the phase where the apartment takes its final shape. We schedule 5-7 days for this phase.|||## Total Timeline|||A complete apartment renovation in Rome takes an average of 45-60 working days. Edilquadro respects the timelines agreed in the contract, with penalties at our expense in case of delays. Contact us for a detailed quote and customized timeline.

blog_art5.title: External Wall Insulation for Condominiums in Rome: Costs, Benefits and Tax Bonuses
blog_art5.meta_title: External Wall Insulation Condominium Rome - Costs & Benefits 2026 | Edilquadro
blog_art5.excerpt: Everything about thermal insulation for condominiums in Rome: costs, benefits, how to access the 65% Ecobonus, and why choose Edilquadro.
blog_art5.content: External wall insulation (cappotto termico) is one of the most effective measures to improve the energy efficiency of a condominium in Rome. It reduces consumption, increases living comfort, and adds property value. Here's everything you need to know.|||## What Is External Wall Insulation|||External wall insulation consists of applying insulating panels to the building's exterior walls, protected by reinforced plaster and an aesthetic finish. In Rome, where summer temperatures often exceed 35°C, thermal insulation reduces both summer heat and winter cold.|||## Benefits of External Insulation|||The benefits are numerous: 30-50% reduction in energy consumption, elimination of thermal bridges and condensation, acoustic comfort improvement, property value increase of 10-15%, energy class improvement by 2-3 levels, and CO2 emission reduction.|||## Costs of External Insulation in Rome|||For a condominium in Rome, external insulation costs range between €60 and €120 per square meter, depending on the insulating material chosen and building complexity. For an average condominium with 1,000 sqm of external surface, the investment ranges between €60,000 and €120,000 including VAT.|||## Insulating Materials Compared|||EPS (expanded polystyrene): economical, good insulation, €60-80/sqm installed. Rock wool: excellent thermal and acoustic insulation, fire resistant, €80-100/sqm. Wood fiber: natural, eco-friendly, excellent summer thermal lag, €90-120/sqm. For Rome we recommend materials with good thermal lag to combat summer heat.|||## 65% Ecobonus for External Insulation|||External insulation qualifies for the Ecobonus with a 65% deduction over 10 years. For a €100,000 project, the tax savings amount to €65,000. Requirements include: pre-intervention energy diagnosis, pre and post-work energy performance certificates, and ENEA notification within 90 days of completion.|||## The Condominium Process|||The condominium assembly must approve the intervention with a majority of attendees representing at least one-third of the building's value. Edilquadro supports the building administrator in preparing technical documentation and presenting the project to the assembly.|||## Why Choose Edilquadro|||We specialize in condominium projects in Rome with compliant scaffolding, qualified and certified personnel, and complete management of all ENEA and tax procedures. Contact us for a free site survey and detailed quote for your condominium.
